title: Plant DNA Extraction and Preparation for ONT Sequencing
author: Boas Pucker
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bcvyiw7w

steps.title: Preheat 5 ml CTAB1 + 300...
steps.instruction: Preheat 5 ml CTAB1 + 300 µL ß-ME in 50 ml tube to 75 C (The amount of ß-ME is working, but was not optimized)

steps.title: Homogenize fresh material with morta and...
steps.instruction: Homogenize fresh material with morta and pestle in liquid N2 (It is important to use fresh material which was not frozen previously. Less than 1g of young leaves is usually sufficient. Keeping plants in the dark for a few days prior to DNA extraction might reduce the amount of starch and the number of plastids.)

steps.title: Add homogenized material (powder) to the...
steps.instruction: Add homogenized material (powder) to the preheated CTAB1 buffer and resuspend carefully (It is crucial to completely resuspend the powder. Avoid transfer of ice.)

steps.title: Incubation for at least 30 minutes...
steps.instruction: Incubation for at least 30 minutes (up to 2 hours possible). Invert tubes frequently to carefully mix the solution.

steps.title: Re-cool to room temperature
steps.instruction: Re-cool to room temperature

steps.title: Add 5 ml dichloromethane and mix...
steps.instruction: Add 5 ml dichloromethane and mix gently by inverting the tube.

steps.title: Centrifugation for 30 minutes at >10,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifugation for 30 minutes at >10,000 x g at room temperature

steps.title: Transfer upper phase carefully to new 50 ml tube
steps.instruction: Transfer upper phase carefully to new 50 ml tube. (Do not disturb interphase. Upper phase should be colorless.)

steps.title: Add 10 ml CTAB2 and mix...
steps.instruction: Add 10 ml CTAB2 and mix gently by inverting the tube.

steps.title: Centrifugation for 30 minutes at >10,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifugation for 30 minutes at >10,000 x g at room temperature

steps.title: Discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Add 2 ml 1M NaCl and re-dissolve pellet
steps.instruction: Add 2 ml 1M NaCl and re-dissolve pellet.

steps.title: Add 2 ml Isopropanol and mix gently
steps.instruction: Add 2 ml Isopropanol and mix gently.

steps.title: Centrifugation for 30 minutes at >10,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifugation for 30 minutes at >10,000 x g at room temperature

steps.title: Discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Wash with 2 ml 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash with 2 ml 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Centrifugation for 10 minutes at >10,000...
steps.instruction: Centrifugation for 10 minutes at >10,000 x g at room temperature

steps.title: Discard supernatant
steps.instruction: Discard supernatant.

steps.title: Brief centrifugation to collect droplets
steps.instruction: Brief centrifugation to collect droplets.

steps.title: Remove droplets
steps.instruction: Remove droplets.

steps.title: Dissolve sediment in 100 µl CTAB-TE...
steps.instruction: Dissolve sediment in 100 µl CTAB-TE via incubation over night at room temperature. (RNase is active during this time)

steps.title: NanoDrop measurement with 1 µl of DNA solution (expecation
steps.instruction: NanoDrop measurement with 1 µl of DNA solution (expecation: several µg/µl)

steps.title: Agarose gel
steps.instruction: Agarose gel: 1 µl DNA + 3 µl loading buffer + 3 µl water (1kb DNA ladder from NEB as size control; any large DNA fragment will do the job)

steps.title: Qubit measurement with 1 µl DNA
steps.instruction: Qubit measurement with 1 µl DNA

steps.title: Removal of short fragments following the SRE (Circulomics) handbook: https://www
steps.instruction: Removal of short fragments following the SRE (Circulomics) handbook: https://www.n-genetics.com/products/1317/1023/17106.pdf

steps.title: LSK109 works best for plant genome sequencing (in my hands)
steps.instruction: LSK109 works best for plant genome sequencing (in my hands). Two libraries may be prepared for the cost of one by starting with 2 µg of DNA (as recommended for R10 flow cells) even when using R9 flow cells. This comes with the risk that not all pores will be occupied depending on the library quality. The volumen of the final step (Elution Buffer) is increased to 25 µl. Half of the library (12 µl) is used for sequencing right away and the rest of the library can be stored at 4 C in a fridge until needed after a nuclease flush. There might be a small decrease in average read length due to storage of the library.

steps.title: Nuclease flush might be necessary after some time (e
steps.instruction: Nuclease flush might be necessary after some time (e.g. 12-24 hours) depending on the DNA quality. Incubation with DNaseI on the flow cell for 2 hours works well in my hands.
